objective: Discover nonlinear patterns, feature interactions, and optimal predictors using ML

results.title: Top predictor → Homicide
results.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
results.finding: Homicide: #1 predictor = graduados_en_programas_stem_xm (importance=0.276). Top 3: graduados_en_programas_st, velocidad_de_descarga_de_, cobertura_de_banda_ancha_
results.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

results.title: Top predictor → Robbery
results.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
results.finding: Robbery: #1 predictor = penetracion_de_banda_ancha_fij (importance=0.225). Top 3: penetracion_de_banda_anch, graduados_en_programas_st, velocidad_de_descarga_de_
results.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

results.title: Top predictor → Fraud
results.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
results.finding: Fraud: #1 predictor = indice_de_desarrollo_digital_e (importance=0.299). Top 3: indice_de_desarrollo_digi, graduados_en_programas_st, penetracion_de_banda_anch
results.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

results.title: Top predictor → Domestic violence
results.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
results.finding: Domestic violence: #1 predictor = empresas_que_utilizan_banca_el (importance=0.324). Top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, cobertura_de_banda_ancha_, graduados_en_programas_st
results.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

results.title: Top predictor → Drug trafficking
results.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
results.finding: Drug trafficking: #1 predictor = empresas_que_utilizan_banca_el (importance=0.415). Top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, graduados_en_programas_st, cobertura_de_redes_movile
results.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

results.title: MI vs Pearson: Average wage
results.description: Mutual information (nonlinear) vs Pearson (linear) feature ranking
results.finding: MI top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, penetracion_de_banda_anch, velocidad_de_descarga_de_. Pearson top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, indice_de_desarrollo_digi, penetracion_de_banda_anch. Overlap=2/3. 1 features are nonlinear-only.
results.recommendation: Features that rank high in MI but not in Pearson have nonlinear relationships.

results.title: MI vs Pearson: Crime rate
results.description: Mutual information (nonlinear) vs Pearson (linear) feature ranking
results.finding: MI top 3: cobertura_de_redes_movile, penetracion_de_banda_anch, cobertura_de_banda_ancha_. Pearson top 3: indice_de_desarrollo_digi, penetracion_de_banda_anch, empresas_que_utilizan_ban. Overlap=1/3. 2 features are nonlinear-only.
results.recommendation: Features that rank high in MI but not in Pearson have nonlinear relationships.

results.title: MI vs Pearson: Homicide rate
results.description: Mutual information (nonlinear) vs Pearson (linear) feature ranking
results.finding: MI top 3: graduados_en_programas_st, cobertura_de_redes_movile, cobertura_de_banda_ancha_. Pearson top 3: graduados_en_programas_st, velocidad_de_descarga_de_, penetracion_de_banda_anch. Overlap=1/3. 2 features are nonlinear-only.
results.recommendation: Features that rank high in MI but not in Pearson have nonlinear relationships.

results.title: MI vs Pearson: Fraud rate
results.description: Mutual information (nonlinear) vs Pearson (linear) feature ranking
results.finding: MI top 3: indice_de_desarrollo_digi, graduados_en_programas_st, penetracion_de_banda_anch. Pearson top 3: indice_de_desarrollo_digi, penetracion_de_banda_anch, empresas_que_utilizan_ban. Overlap=2/3. 1 features are nonlinear-only.
results.recommendation: Features that rank high in MI but not in Pearson have nonlinear relationships.

results.title: Threshold effect: IDDE → Wage
results.description: Piecewise linear regression with optimal breakpoint in IDDE
results.finding: Best threshold=130.5, R²_piecewise=0.214, R²_linear=0.163, Δ=+0.050. Threshold effect detected.
results.recommendation: If threshold found, the dashboard can say "above IDDE=X, the relationship changes."

results.title: Linear vs RF R²: Average wage
results.description: Does RF capture patterns that linear regression misses?
results.finding: Linear R²=-0.068, RF R²=0.214, Δ=+0.282. Nonlinear patterns present.

results.title: Linear vs RF R²: Family trust
results.description: Does RF capture patterns that linear regression misses?
results.finding: Linear R²=-0.443, RF R²=0.146, Δ=+0.589. Nonlinear patterns present.

results.title: Linear vs RF R²: Friend trust
results.description: Does RF capture patterns that linear regression misses?
results.finding: Linear R²=0.109, RF R²=0.462, Δ=+0.354. Nonlinear patterns present.

results.title: Interaction effects → Crime rate
results.description: Polynomial (degree=2, interaction_only) vs linear with 4 features
results.finding: Linear R²=-0.864, With interactions R²=-0.689, Δ=+0.175. N_interactions=6.

results.title: Dose-response quartiles: IDDE → Wage
results.description: Mean of target by IDDE quartile to detect nonlinearity

results.title: Per-cluster LOOCV → Wage
results.description: Does the model predict differently within each digitalization cluster?

results.title: Per-cluster LOOCV → Crime
results.description: Does the model predict differently within each digitalization cluster?

results.title: PDP monotonicity: IDDE → Wage
results.description: Is the partial dependence curve monotonic or does it have reversals?
results.finding: PDP range: [9509.2, 13325.2]. Monotonic=False. 15 increasing steps, 3 decreasing steps.
results.recommendation: Non-monotonic PDP means "more IDDE is not always better" — powerful nuance.

results.title: PDP monotonicity: IDDE → Crime
results.description: Is the partial dependence curve monotonic or does it have reversals?
results.finding: PDP range: [465.9, 2497.3]. Monotonic=False. 14 increasing steps, 4 decreasing steps.
results.recommendation: Non-monotonic PDP means "more IDDE is not always better" — powerful nuance.

results.title: RFE top 3 features → Average wage
results.description: Recursive Feature Elimination: which 3 features matter most?
results.finding: Selected: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, velocidad_de_descarga_de_, indice_de_desarrollo_digi. R²(3 features)=0.180, R²(all)=0.172.

results.title: RFE top 3 features → Crime rate
results.description: Recursive Feature Elimination: which 3 features matter most?
results.finding: Selected: cobertura_de_banda_ancha_, penetracion_de_banda_anch, graduados_en_programas_st. R²(3 features)=-0.511, R²(all)=-0.655.

results.title: RFE top 3 features → Homicide rate
results.description: Recursive Feature Elimination: which 3 features matter most?
results.finding: Selected: cobertura_de_banda_ancha_, graduados_en_programas_st, velocidad_de_descarga_de_. R²(3 features)=-0.384, R²(all)=-0.603.

results.title: Stacking → Average wage
results.description: Stacked ensemble: Linear + RF + GB → meta-learner (LOO)
results.finding: Stacking R²=0.513, best base R²=0.478, Δ=+0.035. Weights: LR=-0.32, RF=0.81, GB=0.44

results.title: Stacking → Crime rate
results.description: Stacked ensemble: Linear + RF + GB → meta-learner (LOO)
results.finding: Stacking R²=0.284, best base R²=0.165, Δ=+0.119. Weights: LR=0.41, RF=-0.35, GB=0.66

results.title: Stacking → Homicide rate
results.description: Stacked ensemble: Linear + RF + GB → meta-learner (LOO)
results.finding: Stacking R²=0.209, best base R²=-0.054, Δ=+0.263. Weights: LR=0.54, RF=-0.94, GB=0.12

results.title: Elastic Net features → Average wage
results.description: Which features survive regularization longest?
results.finding: Top 3 (last to be zeroed): cobertura_de_banda_ancha_(α=2099.2559), penetracion_de_banda_anch(α=2099.2559), cobertura_de_redes_movile(α=2099.2559)

results.title: RF permutation importance → Average wage
results.description: Random Forest with N=31, 200 trees, max_depth=3
results.finding: R²_train=0.883, R²_LOOCV=nan. Top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_banca_el=1.1522±0.2759, penetracion_de_banda_ancha_fij=0.0756±0.0232, velocidad_de_descarga_de_banda=0.0718±0.0169
results.recommendation: If LOOCV R² > 0.2, there are genuine nonlinear patterns. Report feature importances.

results.title: PCA regression → Average wage
results.description: Optimal number of principal components for prediction
results.finding: Best k=4, R²=0.252, variance explained=96.1%. Linear R²(all features) from 3.3 for comparison.

results.title: PCA regression → Crime rate
results.description: Optimal number of principal components for prediction

results.title: High vs low crime state classification
results.description: Can digital features classify states as high/low crime? (median split)
results.finding: Accuracy=0.516, AUC=0.546. Top predictor: graduados_en_programas_stem_xm (0.275). Weak separation.

results.title: GB vs RF: Average wage
